reasons: Karrie pressures Gatotkaca's high durability with repeated Max HP true damage instead of relying only on normal physical damage.
Gatotkaca wants extended melee fights, while Karrie can kite and stack damage safely when protected.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca wants to absorb damage as a frontline tank, while Karrie can stack Lightwheel Mark and convert repeated hits into Max HP true damage.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has frontline protection and can hit Gatotkaca without being jumped.
Karrie keeps spacing instead of fighting inside Gatotkaca's engage zone.
failureCases: Gatotkaca reaches Karrie with Avatar of Guardian or teammate crowd control.
Karrie is forced to hit other targets and cannot safely stack damage on Gatotkaca.

reasons: Lunox can pressure Gatotkaca with magic penetration and burst instead of taking a slow frontline trade.
Her Order and Chaos tools give her defensive and offensive windows against Gatotkaca's engage.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca becomes difficult to kill through normal damage, while Lunox can use penetration and burst windows to damage durable frontliners.
worksBestWhen: Lunox tracks Gatotkaca's ultimate and saves her defensive or escape window for the real engage.
Lunox attacks from a safe mid-range position.
failureCases: Lunox is caught before entering the correct Order or Chaos state.
Gatotkaca forces Lunox to use her defensive window early and re-engages with teammates.

reasons: Valir can push back and slow Gatotkaca, reducing his ability to follow up after engaging.
Valir's self-cleanse and movement speed help him survive the first crowd-control window if timed correctly.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca wants to close distance and keep enemies inside his control zone, while Valir can use pushback, slows, and self-cleanse timing to delay or deny the follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds Searing Torrent until Gatotkaca commits.
Valir plays behind frontline instead of standing in the first engage path.
failureCases: Gatotkaca engages from fog before Valir can react.
Valir wastes his displacement before Gatotkaca commits.

reasons: Dyrroth can punish Gatotkaca in lane or skirmishes with Physical Defense reduction and burst pressure.
This is strongest before Gatotkaca becomes too durable or when Dyrroth has item tempo.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca wants to survive long trades, while Dyrroth can create a defense-reduction burst window that punishes him before he stabilizes.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth fights early or with follow-up damage.
Dyrroth avoids wasting his burst after Gatotkaca has already forced a teamfight.
failureCases: Gatotkaca survives the first burst and turns the fight with taunt or team follow-up.

reasons: Diggie can reduce Gatotkaca's teamfight value by protecting allies from the key crowd-control window.
This matchup denies Gatotkaca's setup rather than killing him directly.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca wants to start fights with area control, while Diggie can protect nearby allies with cleanse, control immunity, and shielding during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Gatotkaca's real commit.
Diggie stays close enough to protect the allies Gatotkaca wants to catch.
failureCases: Gatotkaca baits Time Journey before fully committing.
Gatotkaca catches Diggie away from the teammates who need protection.
